commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Discord-only opt-in for the shared channel SecretRef account surface so a configured top-level Discord token can materialize the implicit default account alongside named accounts.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +38. Total +58 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows a top-level Discord token plus token-owning named accounts can skip default-token materialization on current main, which then reaches Discord startup as configured_unavailable; I did not run a live Discord hot-reload repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of a public plugin SDK helper signature, not a narrow code repair.

target.title: fix(discord): keep default account online when adding named accounts
target.body:
Closes #92985

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with a working default Discord account could take that account offline when adding a second named Discord account backed by SecretRefs. The default account's top-level token could remain unresolved in the active runtime snapshot, causing Discord startup to treat it as configured but unavailable and enter the restart loop reported in #92985.

## Why This Change Was Made

Discord already treats a configured top-level token as an implicit `default` account even when named accounts exist. This change makes the shared channel SecretRef collection surface optionally represent that same implicit default account, and enables the option only for Discord token collection. It keeps sibling channel behavior unchanged and does not add a Discord runtime fallback that would read SecretRefs outside the active snapshot.

## User Impact

Adding a named Discord account no longer prevents the existing default account's SecretRef token from being materialized. Multi-account Discord gateways can keep the default account online while bringing up the additional account.

## Evidence

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/discord/src/secret-config-contract.ts src/secrets/channel-secret-basic-runtime.ts src/secrets/runtime-discord-surface.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/discord/src/secret-config-contract.ts src/secrets/channel-secret-basic-runtime.ts src/secrets/runtime-discord-surface.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.vitest-cache-92985-final OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/secrets/runtime-discord-surface.test.ts src/secrets/runtime-telegram-token-inheritance.test.ts src/secrets/runtime-channel-inactive-variants.test.ts`
  - 3 files passed, 22 tests passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt "Review the narrow fix for issue #92985: Discord top-level token SecretRef should remain active for the implicit default account when named accounts are added. Focus on owner boundary, sibling channel behavior, and whether the shared helper option is the best minimal fix."`
  - clean: no accepted/actionable findings
